creator: martin@lispworks.com
text:
(In reply to Warner Losh from comment #30)
I can repeat it with the attached disk image (in testdisk.tar) by doing:

cd /tmp
tar -x -S -f testdisk.tar
mdconfig -a -t vnode -f testdisk -u 0

This gives:

GEOM_PART: partition 1 contains partition 33: start[1] 0 > start[33] 2032, end[33] 260095 < end[1] 520192
GEOM_PART: integrity check failed (md0s2, EBR)

and the "gpart show md0" output is:

=>     63  2097089  md0  MBR  (1.0G)
       63     1985       - free -  (993K)
     2048   520193    1  linux-data  (254M)
   522241     2047       - free -  (1.0M)
   524288   520193    2  ebr  (254M)
  1044481     2047       - free -  (1.0M)
  1046528   520193    3  linux-data  (254M)
  1566721     2047       - free -  (1.0M)
  1568768   528384    4  linux-data  (258M)

The tar file was created on Debian Linux 11.8 like this:

cd /tmp
rm testdisk
truncate --size 1G testdisk
losetup /dev/loop0 testdisk
parted -s /dev/loop0 -- \
  mklabel msdos \
  mkpart primary 2048s 522240s \
  mkpart extended 524288s 1044480s \
  mkpart primary 1046528s 1566720s \
  mkpart primary 1568768s 2097151s \
  mkpart logical 784384s 1044480s \
  mkpart logical linux-swap 526336s 784383s \
  quit
losetup -d /dev/loop0
tar -c -S -f testdisk.tar testdisk

and "parted -s /dev/loop0 unit s print" prints this for it:

Model: Loopback device (loopback)
Disk /dev/loop0: 2097152s
Sector size (logical/physical): 512B/512B
Partition Table: msdos
Disk Flags: 

Number  Start     End       Size     Type      File system  Flags
 1      2048s     522240s   520193s  primary
 2      524288s   1044480s  520193s  extended               lba
 6      526336s   784383s   258048s  logical
 5      784384s   1044480s  260097s  logical
 3      1046528s  1566720s  520193s  primary
 4      1568768s  2097151s  528384s  primary
